disclaimer: Models AI/ML papers as temporary epistemic states - typed, paper-anchored claims with explicit limitations and open questions - over a deterministic fixture corpus. Exactly one paper is real (the base-paper anchor); every other is explicitly synthetic and illustrative, so nothing here is a fabricated real citation. DESi structures research; it does not rank, score, peer-review, judge truth or debunk. Deterministic and replay-stable.

claims.text: In-context RL can handle variable action spaces and stitch skills.
Exploration can collapse; sparse reward and repetitive trajectories remain open (Section 4.6).
How can exploration be governed without replacing the policy?

metadata.abstract: Studies in-context RL for variable action spaces and skill stitching; Section 4.6 lists open exploration problems.
metadata.title: In-Context Reinforcement Learning for Variable Action Spaces and Skill Stitching

claims.text: A read-only governance layer reduces redundant search on a synthetic corpus.
A generator/governor split increases distinct-state coverage versus a single explorer.
Results are limited to synthetic fixtures.
Does the effect hold under a trained policy?
All reported numbers are replay-exact.

metadata.abstract: Synthetic study of a read-only governance layer over in-context exploration.
metadata.title: Read-Only Exploration Governance for In-Context Agents (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Separating generation and governance preserves exploration diversity on synthetic runs.
Soft re-weighting bounds accumulated authority drift.
Agents are rule-based stand-ins, not trained.
How does the split scale to many agents?

metadata.abstract: Synthetic study of generation/governance separation across multiple agents.
metadata.title: Generator/Governor Splits in Multi-Agent Exploration (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Containment caps high-certainty incoherent paths on synthetic fixtures.
Containment may generalise beyond the fixtures.
Only a synthetic generator is evaluated.
How does containment behave with a real generator?

metadata.abstract: Synthetic study of containment for high-certainty incoherent paths.
metadata.title: Residual Hallucination in Tool-Using LLM Agents (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Structural classification explains soft re-weighting decisions.
The account is post-hoc, not causal.
Can governance be interpreted causally?

metadata.abstract: Synthetic study of structural classification of exploration trajectories.
metadata.title: Interpretability of Exploration Policies (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Read-only constraints avoid hidden optimisation authority.
Non-authoritative layers may reduce misalignment pressure.
No real-world deployment is evaluated.
How do constraints behave under distribution shift?

metadata.abstract: Synthetic position on read-only constraints and optimisation authority.
metadata.title: Alignment Constraints as Read-Only Governance (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Exploration benchmarks are replay-exact under fixed fixtures.
Fragile and reproducible claims differ by fixture sensitivity.
Only a small benchmark suite is covered.
Does reproducibility hold across labs?

metadata.abstract: Synthetic study of replay-exact exploration benchmarks.
metadata.title: Reproducibility of Exploration Benchmarks (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Reasoning chains might guide exploration breadth.
Can reasoning traces be linked to exploration breadth?
No empirical test is provided.

metadata.abstract: Synthetic speculation on reasoning traces and exploration breadth.
metadata.title: Reasoning Chains and Open Exploration Questions (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Synthetic study H8 presents an empirical observation about Multi-Agent exploration using trajectory_clustering on a synthetic corpus.
A comparative observation links generator_governor_split to coverage_ratio on the synthetic corpus.
Results are limited to a synthetic fixture corpus and are not evaluated on real systems.
Whether the Multi-Agent effect holds beyond the synthetic corpus remains an open question.

metadata.abstract: Synthetic illustrative study H8 on Multi-Agent exploration using trajectory_clustering over a synthetic corpus.
metadata.title: Synthetic Study H8: Multi-Agent / ML Exploration (synthetic illustrative)

claims.text: Synthetic study H9 reports an experimental result on Reasoning exploration using uncertainty_gating on a synthetic corpus.
A reproducibility observation links soft_reweighting to containment_rate on the synthetic corpus.
Results are limited to a synthetic fixture corpus and are not evaluated on real systems.
Whether the Reasoning effect holds beyond the synthetic corpus remains an open question.

metadata.abstract: Synthetic illustrative study H9 on Reasoning exploration using uncertainty_gating over a synthetic corpus.
metadata.title: Synthetic Study H9: Reasoning / LLM Exploration (synthetic illustrative)
